packanOrderCodes.title: Pack an Order Codes
packanOrderCodes.intro: Check the current Pack an Order code status and learn where future reward codes may be announced. We track every official source so you know exactly when working codes appear.

items.label: No active codes
items.description: No working Pack an Order codes are published in the official game description or developer community right now.

items.label: No redeem menu listed
items.description: The official instructions do not currently mention a Codes, Gift, Twitter, or Redeem button inside Pack an Order.

items.label: Official update sources
items.description: Watch the Roblox game description and the developer community for newly released Pack an Order codes and reward events.

items.label: Code troubleshooting
items.description: When codes arrive, enter them exactly as published, remove extra spaces, confirm the code has not expired, and make sure it has not already been used.

packanOrderBeginnerGuide.title: Pack an Order Beginner Guide
packanOrderBeginnerGuide.intro: Learn the complete warehouse workflow, from reading customer orders to loading finished boxes onto delivery trucks. Faster completions earn Coins, improve your leaderboard time, and help you unlock upgrades and abilities.

steps.title: Choose Solo or Multiplayer
steps.description: Enter the warehouse alone or join friends in Pack an Order. Teams can divide the shelves and pack several parts of an order at the same time.

steps.title: Read the Customer Order
steps.description: Check which toys are required before collecting anything. Press R to cycle between available orders.

steps.title: Search the Shelves
steps.description: Move through the warehouse and locate products that match the active order. Learning the shelf layout reduces wasted travel time.

steps.title: Inspect the Product
steps.description: Use Right Click or the Y button to inspect an item when similar products are difficult to distinguish.

steps.title: Pick Up the Correct Item
steps.description: Press E or Left Click to collect a product. Use the scroll wheel to switch between items when carrying more than one.

steps.title: Pack the Order
steps.description: Bring the matching product to its order box and press E or Left Click to pack it. Press Q to drop an incorrect or unwanted item.

steps.title: Load the Delivery Truck
steps.description: Complete every requested item, finish the box, and load the packed order onto the delivery truck.

steps.title: Spend Coins on Upgrades
steps.description: Use earned Coins to improve Speed, Reach, and Carry Capacity. Repeat orders to unlock abilities and compete for faster leaderboard times.

quickTips: Read the full ticket before leaving the packing area so you grab every required product in one route.
Inspect similar-looking toys before packing to avoid placing the wrong item in a box.
Cycle carried products with the scroll wheel and pack them in the order shown on the ticket.
Drop incorrect items with Q near their original shelf instead of carrying them across the warehouse.

packanOrderBestUpgrades.title: Best Upgrades in Pack an Order
packanOrderBestUpgrades.intro: Compare Speed, Carry Capacity, and Reach to decide where to spend your Coins first. The best upgrade depends on how much time you lose while moving, carrying products, or interacting with shelves.

items.effect: Improves movement through the warehouse and reduces travel time between shelves, packing stations, and delivery trucks.
items.recommendation: Buy Speed first because movement is required during every stage of an order.

items.effect: Allows more products to be transported before returning to an order box.
items.bestFor: Solo players and orders with products from several shelves
items.recommendation: Prioritize it in solo play to reduce repeated trips. Use the scroll wheel to cycle through carried items.

items.effect: Extends the distance from which products and packing targets can be interacted with.
items.bestFor: Fast shelf collection and coordinated warehouse routes
items.recommendation: Upgrade Reach after establishing good movement speed. It is especially useful when teammates divide the warehouse into sections.

items.effect: Balances movement, collection efficiency, and the number of trips required per order.
items.soloPriority: Speed to Carry Capacity to Reach
items.teamPriority: Speed to Reach to Carry Capacity
items.recommendation: Solo players benefit more from carrying several products, while organized teams can focus on movement and quick shelf interactions.

packanOrderControls.title: Pack an Order Controls
packanOrderControls.intro: Use these controls to collect products, inspect items, manage orders, and pack boxes without slowing down. The most important inputs are E or Left Click for collecting and packing, R for switching orders, and the scroll wheel for managing carried products.

items.action: Pick up or pack an item
items.tip: Check the active order before interacting so the wrong product is not placed into a box.
Drop incorrect products near their original shelf instead of carrying them across the warehouse.
Select an order before entering a shelf section and collect several matching products during the same route.
Switch to the required product before approaching its packing box.
Inspect products with similar shapes or colors before packing them.

packanOrderAbilitiesGuide.title: Pack an Order Abilities and Upgrades
packanOrderAbilitiesGuide.intro: Pack an Order includes unlockable abilities alongside three core upgrades: Speed, Reach, and Carry Capacity. Prioritize upgrades that remove the biggest delay from your current route, then use abilities to reduce searching, carrying, or packing time further.

items.name: Speed
items.effect: Improves how quickly you move between shelves, packing stations, and delivery trucks.
items.bestFor: Large warehouse routes and repeated item runs
items.recommendedTiming: Strong first upgrade for most players
items.usageTip: Use the extra movement speed to collect items from distant shelves before returning to the packing area.

items.name: Carry Capacity
items.effect: Allows more requested products to be carried during one shelf run.
items.bestFor: Orders containing several products or repeated quantities
items.recommendedTiming: Prioritize when inventory limits regularly force extra trips
items.usageTip: Cycle carried items with the scroll wheel and pack them in the order shown on the active ticket.

items.name: Reach
items.effect: Extends the effective interaction range used while collecting and packing products.
items.bestFor: Dense shelves and items positioned slightly outside normal pickup range
items.recommendedTiming: Purchase after movement and carrying limits are under control
items.usageTip: Approach the correct shelf section and use the added reach to reduce small positioning adjustments.

items.name: Unlockable Abilities
items.effect: Adds tools designed to make the order-packing process faster.
items.bestFor: Reducing delays during more demanding timed orders
items.usageTip: Choose abilities that address your slowest task: finding products, transporting them, or packing completed orders.

packanOrderOrdersAndItems.title: Pack an Order Orders and Items Guide
packanOrderOrdersAndItems.intro: Each customer order lists the exact products and quantities that must be collected from the warehouse shelves. Check every item before packing it, cycle between active orders when needed, and load completed boxes onto the delivery trucks.

items.heading: Read the Customer Ticket
items.content: The order ticket displays the customer, order number, product names, required quantities, and an item checklist.
controls: Press R to cycle between active orders.
items.example: An order for Becca may request 1 Red Squishy, 2 Matcha Dumplings, 1 Clicker, and 3 Salted Butter items.

items.heading: Match Names and Quantities Exactly
items.content: Collect the named shelf product and continue until the requested quantity is satisfied. Similar-looking toys should be checked by name before they are placed in the box.
controls: Use E or Left Click to pick up and pack an item.
Use Q to drop the currently selected item.
items.example: A request for two Matcha Dumplings requires two matching items; one item does not complete that checklist entry.

items.heading: Inspect an Item Before Packing
items.content: Inspection helps confirm that the carried product matches the active customer order before it is added to the box.
controls: Use Right Click or Y to inspect an item.
items.example: Inspect products when their color, shape, or shelf position makes them easy to confuse with another item.

items.heading: Manage Carried Products
items.content: Players can hold products while moving between shelf sections and the packing station. Select the correct carried product before attempting to pack it.
controls: Use the scroll wheel to cycle through carried items.
items.example: Collecting several products during one route becomes more efficient after increasing Carry Capacity.

items.heading: Finish and Load the Order
items.content: After every listed product has been packed correctly, move the completed box through the warehouse workflow and load it onto the delivery truck.
controls: Use E or Left Click for pickup and packing interactions.
items.example: The order is complete only after the required products have been packed and the delivery step has been finished.

packanOrderCoinsAndLeveling.title: Pack an Order Coins and Leveling Guide
packanOrderCoinsAndLeveling.intro: Coins are earned through the order-completion loop and are used to unlock progression upgrades. Since Pack an Order rewards efficient packing and tracks completion times, the most reliable farming method is to finish correct orders repeatedly while improving Speed, Carry Capacity, and Reach.

items.goal: Learn the warehouse layout and complete orders correctly
items.actions: Read one ticket at a time, inspect uncertain products, pack the listed quantities, and load the completed box.
items.spendPriority: Save the first Coins for a core upgrade
items.progressionValue: Correct, repeatable completions create a dependable Coin loop.

items.goal: Remove the largest source of wasted time
items.actions: Choose Speed when travel is slow, Carry Capacity when extra trips are common, or Reach when shelf interactions require frequent repositioning.
items.spendPriority: Buy the upgrade that fixes the current bottleneck
items.progressionValue: A targeted upgrade improves every order that follows.

items.goal: Collect several requested products during each shelf route
items.actions: Review the full ticket before leaving the packing area, group nearby shelf items, and cycle carried products with the scroll wheel.
items.spendPriority: Continue improving Speed and Carry Capacity
items.progressionValue: Fewer return trips allow more orders to be completed during the same play session.

items.goal: Add tools that shorten searching, carrying, or packing tasks
items.actions: Unlock abilities as they become available and use them where they remove the most time from the order workflow.
items.spendPriority: Balance ability unlocks with permanent core upgrades
items.progressionValue: Abilities supplement the basic upgrade system and support faster completion times.

items.goal: Complete full order sets as quickly and consistently as possible
items.actions: Memorize shelf locations, avoid incorrect products, plan routes before moving, and minimize idle time at boxes and trucks.
items.spendPriority: Improve any remaining weak core stat
items.progressionValue: Faster repeatable runs increase completed-order throughput and improve leaderboard performance.

packanOrderCoopAndLeaderboard.title: Pack an Order Co-op and Leaderboard Strategy
packanOrderCoopAndLeaderboard.intro: Pack an Order supports both solo play and teams of friends, with leaderboards based on fast completion times. Organized crews perform best when players divide shelf searching, packing, and truck-loading duties instead of following the same route.

items.setup: One player handles the full workflow
items.execution: Read the complete ticket before moving, collect nearby products in batches, inspect uncertain items, and use Carry Capacity to reduce return trips.
items.mistakeToAvoid: Running back to the packing station after collecting only one item when more requested products are nearby.

items.setup: Split collection from packing and delivery
roles: Player 1 searches shelves and delivers requested products
Player 2 verifies items, packs boxes, and loads trucks
items.execution: The collector announces the order being handled while the packing player keeps the box and delivery area moving.
items.mistakeToAvoid: Both players leaving the packing station to search for the same item.

items.setup: Assign one player to each major warehouse task
roles: Packer matches products to tickets and fills boxes
Loader moves completed orders to delivery trucks
items.execution: Keep each role active continuously and rotate only when one stage develops a backlog.
items.mistakeToAvoid: Allowing completed boxes to wait while every player searches the shelves.

items.setup: Assign a specific order or shelf zone to each collector
roles: Call out customer names or order numbers
Report completed quantities before changing tasks
items.execution: Separate assignments by active order or warehouse section so each player has a clear target.
items.mistakeToAvoid: Collecting duplicate products after another teammate has already completed the same checklist entry.

items.setup: Use a practiced route and fixed team responsibilities
roles: Fastest player handles long shelf routes
Most accurate player verifies and packs
Available teammate keeps delivery trucks supplied
items.execution: Memorize common item locations, start moving as soon as orders appear, and avoid unnecessary role changes during the run.
items.mistakeToAvoid: Prioritizing movement speed over order accuracy and packing incorrect products.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Pack an Order

questions.question: Is Pack an Order multiplayer?
questions.answer: Yes. Pack an Order is a co-op warehouse game on Roblox. You can play solo or team up with friends to find toys, pack orders, and load delivery trucks faster.

questions.question: What are the core upgrades in Pack an Order?
questions.answer: The three core upgrades in Pack an Order are Speed, Reach, and Carry Capacity. They permanently improve your worker and help you complete orders much faster.

questions.question: Where can I play Pack an Order?
questions.answer: Pack an Order is available for free on Roblox. You can play it on PC, mobile, Xbox, and any other device that supports Roblox.

questions.question: Does Pack an Order have codes?
questions.answer: Pack an Order is newly launched and an official codes system has not been confirmed yet. We track the codes status and will list working Pack an Order codes as soon as they are released.

questions.question: Does Pack an Order support controllers?
questions.answer: Roblox supports controllers on compatible platforms, but keyboard and mouse or touch are the smoothest options for quickly grabbing and packing items in Pack an Order.

questions.question: Will Pack an Order receive updates?
questions.answer: Yes. As a live Roblox game, Pack an Order is expected to receive updates with new items, abilities, upgrades, and balance changes over time.
